inputs.label: Bottleneck Utilization (%)
inputs.businessContext: Current utilization rate of the primary bottleneck resource, measured over a representative period (e.g., last 4 weeks).

businessContext_i18n.en: Current utilization rate of the primary bottleneck resource, measured over a representative period (e.g., last 4 weeks).

inputs.label: Average Demand Rate (units/hour)
inputs.businessContext: Average customer demand rate for the product family flowing through the bottleneck.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average customer demand rate for the product family flowing through the bottleneck.

inputs.label: Bottleneck Capacity (units/hour)
inputs.businessContext: Maximum theoretical output rate of the bottleneck resource under ideal conditions.

businessContext_i18n.en: Maximum theoretical output rate of the bottleneck resource under ideal conditions.

inputs.label: Proposed Investment Cost ($)
inputs.businessContext: Total capital expenditure required for the bottleneck improvement (e.g., new equipment, tooling, automation).

businessContext_i18n.en: Total capital expenditure required for the bottleneck improvement (e.g., new equipment, tooling, automation).

inputs.label: Expected Capacity Increase (%)
inputs.businessContext: Expected percentage increase in bottleneck capacity after the investment.

businessContext_i18n.en: Expected percentage increase in bottleneck capacity after the investment.

inputs.label: Operating Hours per Year
inputs.businessContext: Total planned operating hours of the bottleneck resource per year.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total planned operating hours of the bottleneck resource per year.

inputs.label: Profit Margin per Unit ($)
inputs.businessContext: Contribution margin (revenue minus variable cost) per unit of product flowing through the bottleneck.

businessContext_i18n.en: Contribution margin (revenue minus variable cost) per unit of product flowing through the bottleneck.

inputs.label: Quality Yield at Bottleneck (%)
inputs.businessContext: First-pass yield (percentage of good units) at the bottleneck operation.

businessContext_i18n.en: First-pass yield (percentage of good units) at the bottleneck operation.

rules.message: Demand rate exceeds current bottleneck capacity; investment is critical.
Investment cost must be greater than zero.
Expected capacity increase cannot be negative.
Quality yield below 50% indicates severe quality issues; consider root cause analysis before investment.

thresholds.message: Negative ROI – investment is not financially justified.
Payback period exceeds 5 years; consider alternative investments.
Bottleneck utilization below 70% – consider if this is the true bottleneck.
Negative capacity gap – demand already exceeds capacity; investment is urgent.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
